hypothesis: Applying class-balancing strategies during training—specifically oversampling rare reaction templates or using template-frequency-weighted loss functions—will disproportionately improve out-of-distribution (OOD) generalization to unseen templates in template-free models such as MEGAN and RootAligned, increasing their Pistachio OOD top-10 accuracy by at least fivefold compared to standard training, because the current near-zero OOD performance is driven primarily by the models learning to replicate the skewed template frequency distribution rather than learning underlying chemical transformation principles.
